---
title: "Chapter 14 - Guna-Traya Vibhaga Yoga"
---

<div class="chapter-title-sanskrit">गुणत्रयविभागयोगः</div>

<nav class="chapter-nav"><a href="chapter-13.html">Previous chapter</a><a href="chapter-15.html">Next chapter</a></nav>

<div class="verse-list">
<article class="verse" id="v-14-1" data-reference="14.1">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-1">14.1</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I shall declare again the supreme knowledge, the best of all knowledge, by knowing which all the sages have passed from here to the highest perfection.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">परं भूयः प्रवक्ष्यामि ज्ञानानां ज्ञानमुत्तमम् ।<br>
यज्ज्ञात्वा मुनयः सर्वे परां सिद्धिमितो गताः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-2" data-reference="14.2">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-2">14.2</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Taking refuge in this knowledge, they come to share my nature; they are not born at creation, nor troubled at dissolution.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">इदं ज्ञानमुपाश्रित्य मम साधर्म्यमागताः ।<br>
सर्गेऽपि नोपजायन्ते प्रलये न व्यथन्ति च ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-3" data-reference="14.3">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-3">14.3</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The great <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span> is my womb; in it I place the seed,<br>
and from it, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharata</span>, comes the birth of all beings.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मम योनिर्महद् ब्रह्म तस्मिन्गर्भं दधाम्यहम् ।<br>
सम्भवः सर्वभूतानां ततो भवति भारत ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-4" data-reference="14.4">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-4">14.4</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Whatever forms take birth in all wombs, O son of Kunti, the great <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span> is their womb, and I am the father who gives the seed.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सर्वयोनिषु कौन्तेय मूर्तयः सम्भवन्ति याः ।<br>
तासां ब्रह्म महद्योनिरहं बीजप्रदः पिता ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-5" data-reference="14.5">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-5">14.5</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en"><span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">Sattva</span>, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajas</span>, and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamas</span>—the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span> born of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." aria-label="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." title="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." data-glossary-id="prakriti">prakriti</span>—bind fast in the body, O mighty-armed one, the imperishable embodied one.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सत्त्वं रजस्तम इति गुणाः प्रकृतिसम्भवाः ।<br>
निबध्नन्ति महाबाहो देहे देहिनमव्ययम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-6" data-reference="14.6">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-6">14.6</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Of these, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattva</span>, being pure, is luminous and free of ill; it binds, O blameless one, by attachment to happiness and to knowledge.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तत्र सत्त्वं निर्मलत्वात्प्रकाशकमनामयम् ।<br>
सुखसङ्गेन बध्नाति ज्ञानसङ्गेन चानघ ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-7" data-reference="14.7">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-7">14.7</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Know <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajas</span> to be of the nature of passion, born of craving and attachment; it binds the embodied one, O son of Kunti, by attachment to action.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">रजो रागात्मकं विद्धि तृष्णासङ्गसमुद्भवम् ।<br>
तन्निबध्नाति कौन्तेय कर्मसङ्गेन देहिनम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-8" data-reference="14.8">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-8">14.8</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But know <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamas</span>, born of ignorance, to delude all embodied beings;<br>
it binds them by carelessness, sloth, and sleep, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharata</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तमस्त्वज्ञानजं विद्धि मोहनं सर्वदेहिनाम् ।<br>
प्रमादालस्यनिद्राभिस्तन्निबध्नाति भारत ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-9" data-reference="14.9">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-9">14.9</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en"><span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">Sattva</span> binds one to happiness, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajas</span> to action, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharata</span>; but <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamas</span>, veiling knowledge, binds one to carelessness.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सत्त्वं सुखे सञ्जयति रजः कर्मणि भारत ।<br>
ज्ञानमावृत्य तु तमः प्रमादे सञ्जयत्युत ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-10" data-reference="14.10">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-10">14.10</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en"><span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">Sattva</span> prevails by mastering <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajas</span> and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamas</span>, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharata</span>; <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajas</span>, by mastering <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattva</span> and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamas</span>; and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamas</span> likewise, by mastering <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattva</span> and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajas</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">रजस्तमश्चाभिभूय सत्त्वं भवति भारत ।<br>
रजः सत्त्वं तमश्चैव तमः सत्त्वं रजस्तथा ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-11" data-reference="14.11">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-11">14.11</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">When the light of knowledge shines forth through all the gates of this body, then one should know that <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattva</span> has grown strong.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सर्वद्वारेषु देहेऽस्मिन्प्रकाश उपजायते ।<br>
ज्ञानं यदा तदा विद्याद्विवृद्धं सत्त्वमित्युत ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-12" data-reference="14.12">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-12">14.12</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Greed, busy activity, the undertaking of works, restlessness, and longing—these arise when <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajas</span> has grown strong, O best of the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharatas</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">लोभः प्रवृत्तिरारम्भः कर्मणामशमः स्पृहा ।<br>
रजस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे भरतर्षभ ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-13" data-reference="14.13">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-13">14.13</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Darkness, inertia, carelessness, and mere delusion—these arise when <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamas</span> has grown strong, O joy of the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Kuru: The Kuru lineage or its members; context may refer to the wider dynasty or the Kaurava side." aria-label="Kuru: The Kuru lineage or its members; context may refer to the wider dynasty or the Kaurava side." title="Kuru: The Kuru lineage or its members; context may refer to the wider dynasty or the Kaurava side." data-glossary-id="kuru">Kurus</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अप्रकाशोऽप्रवृत्तिश्च प्रमादो मोह एव च ।<br>
तमस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे कुरुनन्दन ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-14" data-reference="14.14">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-14">14.14</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">When the embodied one meets death while <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattva</span> prevails, he attains the spotless worlds of those who know the highest.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यदा सत्त्वे प्रवृद्धे तु प्रलयं याति देहभृत् ।<br>
तदोत्तमविदां लोकानमलान्प्रतिपद्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-15" data-reference="14.15">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-15">14.15</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Meeting death in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajas</span>, he is born among those attached to action;<br>
and dying in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamas</span>, he is born in the wombs of the deluded.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">रजसि प्रलयं गत्वा कर्मसङ्गिषु जायते ।<br>
तथा प्रलीनस्तमसि मूढयोनिषु जायते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-16" data-reference="14.16">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-16">14.16</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The fruit of good action, they say, is of the nature of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattva</span> and untainted; but the fruit of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajas</span> is pain, and the fruit of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamas</span> is ignorance.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कर्मणः सुकृतस्याहुः सात्त्विकं निर्मलं फलम् ।<br>
रजसस्तु फलं दुःखमज्ञानं तमसः फलम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-17" data-reference="14.17">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-17">14.17</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">From <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattva</span> is born knowledge, from <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajas</span> greed, and from <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamas</span> come carelessness, delusion, and ignorance.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सत्त्वात्सञ्जायते ज्ञानं रजसो लोभ एव च ।<br>
प्रमादमोहौ तमसो भवतोऽज्ञानमेव च ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-18" data-reference="14.18">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-18">14.18</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Those settled in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattva</span> rise upward; those in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajas</span> stay in the middle; those in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamas</span>, settled in the lowest disposition, sink downward.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ऊर्ध्वं गच्छन्ति सत्त्वस्था मध्ये तिष्ठन्ति राजसाः ।<br>
जघन्यगुणवृत्तिस्था अधो गच्छन्ति तामसाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-19" data-reference="14.19">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-19">14.19</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">When the seer perceives no doer other than the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span>, and knows what is beyond the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span>, he attains my being.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">नान्यं गुणेभ्यः कर्तारं यदा द्रष्टानुपश्यति ।<br>
गुणेभ्यश्च परं वेत्ति मद्भावं सोऽधिगच्छति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-20" data-reference="14.20">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-20">14.20</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Passing beyond these three <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span>, which are the source of the body, the embodied one is freed from birth, death, old age, and sorrow, and attains the deathless.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">गुणानेतानतीत्य त्रीन्देही देहसमुद्भवान् ।<br>
जन्ममृत्युजरादुःखैर्विमुक्तोऽमृतमश्नुते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-21" data-reference="14.21">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-21">14.21</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">By what marks is the one who has passed beyond these three <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span> known, O lord? How does he act, and how does he cross beyond these three <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span>?</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कैर्लिङ्गैस्त्रीन्गुणानेतानतीतो भवति प्रभो ।<br>
किमाचारः कथं चैतांस्त्रीन्गुणानतिवर्तते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-22" data-reference="14.22">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-22">14.22</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He neither shuns light, activity, or delusion when they come, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." aria-label="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." title="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." data-glossary-id="pandava">Pandava</span>, nor longs for them when they go;</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">प्रकाशं च प्रवृत्तिं च मोहमेव च पाण्डव ।<br>
न द्वेष्टि सम्प्रवृत्तानि न निवृत्तानि काङ्क्षति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-23" data-reference="14.23">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-23">14.23</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">seated apart, as it were, unmoved by the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span>, he stands firm and does not waver, knowing that it is only the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span> at work;</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">उदासीनवदासीनो गुणैर्यो न विचाल्यते ।<br>
गुणा वर्तन्त इत्येवं योऽवतिष्ठति नेङ्गते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-24" data-reference="14.24">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-24">14.24</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">alike in pain and pleasure, self-possessed, holding clod, stone, and gold as one, the same toward the dear and the unwelcome, steadfast, the same under blame and praise;</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">समदुःखसुखः स्वस्थः समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चनः ।<br>
तुल्यप्रियाप्रियो धीरस्तुल्यनिन्दात्मसंस्तुतिः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-25" data-reference="14.25">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-25">14.25</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">the same in honor and dishonor, the same toward friend and foe, giving up all undertakings—he is said to have gone beyond the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मानापमानयोस्तुल्यस्तुल्यो मित्रारिपक्षयोः ।<br>
सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी गुणातीतः स उच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-26" data-reference="14.26">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-26">14.26</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">And he who serves me with unswerving devotion, passing beyond these <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span>, becomes fit to become <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मां च योऽव्यभिचारेण भक्तियोगेन सेवते ।<br>
स गुणान्समतीत्यैतान्ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-14-27" data-reference="14.27">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-14-27">14.27</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For I am the ground of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>, of the deathless and imperishable, of eternal <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." aria-label="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." title="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." data-glossary-id="dharma">dharma</span>, and of absolute bliss.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ब्रह्मणो हि प्रतिष्ठाहममृतस्याव्ययस्य च ।<br>
शाश्वतस्य च धर्मस्य सुखस्यैकान्तिकस्य च ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

</div>
